items.title: Define y before choosing algorithms
items.avoid: Selecting linear vs logistic regression first and forcing y to fit the algorithm.
items.prefer: Write the exact target definition (continuous quantity vs class label) and only then pick algorithm, loss, and metrics.
items.why: Problem type is a modeling commitment; reversing it late wastes labels and produces misleading metrics.
items.example: Target = "days until churn" (regression/survival) vs "churned within 90 days" (binary classification) — different y, different evaluation.

items.title: Align loss, head, and metrics
items.avoid: Training with MSE on class IDs while reporting accuracy on rounded outputs.
items.prefer: Regression: linear/real head + MSE/MAE + RMSE. Classification: sigmoid/softmax + cross-entropy + precision/recall/F1/AUC.
items.why: Each stage must speak the same target language or gradients and reports optimize the wrong objective.
items.example: PyTorch: nn.MSELoss + nn.Linear(…,1) for price; nn.CrossEntropyLoss + nn.Linear(…,K) for K-way classes.

items.title: Document threshold policy for classifiers
items.avoid: Shipping default 0.5 probability cutoff without checking precision/recall on validation data.
items.prefer: Tune threshold on a labeled validation set to match business costs (false positive vs false negative).
items.why: Classification decisions happen after thresholding; the same model changes behavior with the cutoff.
items.example: Fraud: lower threshold to catch more fraud (higher recall) if manual review cost is acceptable.

items.title: Handle gray zones explicitly
items.avoid: Silently treating star ratings or grades as either pure regression or naive multi-class without stating assumptions.
items.prefer: State whether order matters (ordinal), whether distances between labels are meaningful, and pick loss accordingly.
items.why: Ordinal targets violate both naive classification (no spacing) and naive regression (equal spacing assumed).
items.example: 1–5 survey scores: ordinal logistic or regression with bounded output — document the choice in the model card.
